position_summary: Claude's position is directionally right: the register targets the real judging risks, and this cut would likely be in top-6 contention because it has a rare, concrete scientific judgment moment: IL2 -> UNTESTED, plus an unusually credible self-review beat. The rendered frames make one thing sharper than the text-only panel could: the first 54 seconds are elegant but generic, while the strongest differentiator is visually delayed and then partially buried by dense receipt text, so the video is more vulnerable to triage drop-off than the register's weighted score implies.

findings.title: Video-Visible: Strongest Differentiator Arrives Too Late
findings.evidence: Frames 1-4 consume the title, two-floods setup, matrix problem, and generic Swanson ABC explainer before the first actual Claude Science/live proof frame appears at frame 5, around 1:12. The confident-NO hero frame is frame 7, around 1:44.
findings.concern_path: Async judges triage many 3-minute submissions. If they decide in the first 30-45 seconds, they see a polished but familiar science-communication preamble rather than the unique refusal/referee behavior.
findings.suggested_action: Execute the panel's cold-open idea, but minimally: start with 5-8 seconds of frame 7's IL2 -> UNTESTED refusal, then title, then compress frames 2-4 by one sentence total. Preserve the honest refusal spine; do not replace it with a flashier discovery claim.

findings.title: Video-Visible: Live UI Frames Are Too Small To Read
findings.evidence: Frames 5, 6, and 10 show Claude Science screenshots with dense receipt text. In frame 5 the right receipt table is only partly readable; frame 6 is mostly conversation context; frame 10's key STAT6 interpretation is tiny paragraph text.
findings.concern_path: The narration says these are live, receipt-backed checks, but a judge watching at laptop scale may not be able to verify the receipts visually. That weakens Demo and Claude Use even though the underlying evidence is strong.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the live screenshots, but add one clean zoom/callout per live frame: frame 5 highlights generated candidate questions + live HTTP counts; frame 6 highlights corrected cached/full-sweep wording; frame 10 highlights STAT6 +0.09, adj_p 0.79 and NAB2 self -3.08. Avoid adding more prose outside the callout.

findings.title: Video-Visible: NAB2 Receipt Row Is Over-Dense
findings.evidence: Frame 8 contains 3,935, 22,039, 30, 21,995, rank #4, AB, BC, AC_LIT, AC_KNOWN, EFFECT, SCORE, ALL_PASS, cache delta, and a verdict, all on one shot.
findings.concern_path: The frame is meant to be the payoff, but the viewer's eye is split between too many numbers. The central message, 22,039 -> 30 and NAB2 supported, competes with secondary statistics that only a repo reviewer can interpret.
findings.suggested_action: Accept the panel's de-numbering fix: keep 22,039 -> 30, NAB2 -> Th1/Th2 -> atopic eczema, ac_lit=6, effect=301, verdict=supported, and ALL_PASS/cache delta. Move AB/BC/AC_KNOWN/score/rank into the receipt artifact or a tiny footnote only if legible.

findings.title: Video-Visible: Frame 11 Is Strong But Needs More Isolation
findings.evidence: Frame 11 has the strongest Claude-use trophy: reviewer model flags 'validated' and 'definitive'. It is visually legible, but it appears late at 2:23 after several other dense proof frames.
findings.concern_path: Judges may remember the science result but miss that Claude Science is not just a wrapper: it authored, ran, and criticized the work. This costs Claude Use points, where the project should be unusually competitive.
findings.suggested_action: Execute the panel's fix to give the reviewer catch its own beat. Add a short spoken/on-screen phrase: 'Anthropic's own scientific workbench checked the claim and forced calibration.' Keep the exact flagged terms visible.

findings.title: Video-Visible: STAT6 Caption Is Mostly Good But 'Hardest Confounder' Is Risky
findings.evidence: Frame 10 caption says 'A possible STAT6 cis-artifact — refuted live; the GWAS disease label stays a nomination.' The narration says 'Its hardest confounder — a possible STAT6 cis-artifact — was refuted live...'
findings.concern_path: The visual caption correctly scopes the disease label as a nomination, but the spoken 'hardest confounder' can imply the whole disease-label confounding problem was cleared. A skeptical disease judge may penalize that overreach.
findings.suggested_action: Soften the narration to 'One key confounder...' or 'One possible cis-artifact...' and keep the caption's current scoped wording. Do not say 'confounder cleared.'

findings.title: Video-Visible: Screenshot Captions Are Doing Too Much Work
findings.evidence: Frame 6 lower-third reads: 'full 22,039 sweep = CS-native cached-receipt replay · live proof = micro-sweep · falsification = STAT6 S3'. It is accurate but conceptually dense.
findings.concern_path: This honesty line is important, but the abbreviations and equals-chain may read as defensive or opaque. Judges may not parse which thing was live, cached, or falsified.
findings.suggested_action: Rewrite as two shorter captions held across frames 5-8: 'Live micro-sweep proves the generator and receipt path.' Then on the funnel: 'Full 22,039 sweep is a cached receipt replay; same deterministic referee.'

findings.title: Register Critique: R01 Hook Is Useful But Not As A Full Replacement For The Current Opener
findings.evidence: The panel ranks the R01-rejection hook as Tier 1, and the current frames 1-4 establish a broad scientist dilemma with polished visual continuity.
findings.concern_path: A heavy grant-rejection cold open could sound insider-ish or self-referential if it displaces the universal 'two floods' setup. The video needs stakes, but not a detour into biography before the viewer knows the instrument.
findings.suggested_action: Use the R01 critique as a single sentence after the refusal cold-open or in the written summary: 'The critique was that LBD generates hypotheses almost none follow up; this builds the follow-up test beside generation.' Do not spend a full beat on NIH context.

findings.title: Register Critique: README And Artifact Hygiene Really Are Tier 0
findings.evidence: Panel reports stale README, 'validated' still in shipped code/artifacts, stale Th1/Th2 label, personal paths, license wording, and missing publish attribution. These are repo-facing issues outside the frames but directly affect async judging.
findings.concern_path: A judge impressed by the video may open the repo and find contradictions to the calibrated-honesty claim. That is a high-cost own-goal because the project's trust thesis depends on exact wording and reproducibility.
findings.suggested_action: Do these before public release, exactly as Tier 0 says. Especially remove 'validated' from code/artifacts and replace with 're-derived' or 'receipt-backed', then run a repo-wide text search for banned certainty terms and personal paths.

findings.title: Register Critique: Disease-Hop Boundary Should Be In Video, Not Only Summary
findings.evidence: Frame 8 says 'NAB2 -> Th1/Th2 polarization -> atopic eczema' and 'referee verdict supported'; frame 10 says the disease label remains a nomination. The panel notes HOP-3 is inherited Open Targets/enrichment, not the referee's own causal statistic.
findings.concern_path: The video mostly handles this, but frame 8's 'supported' can still be read as supporting the full disease causal claim. The later STAT6 nomination caveat may not fully attach to the earlier payoff.
findings.suggested_action: On frame 8, change 'referee verdict supported' to 'program hop supported; disease label nominated' or add a tiny split label: 'Perturb-seq-backed: NAB2 -> Th1/Th2' and 'Inherited nomination: eczema'.

findings.title: Harmful-Fix Warning: Do Not Over-Correct Away From The 'Library And Lab' Spine
findings.evidence: Frames 1 and 12 bookend the submission with 'The library and the lab, on one bench' and 'Generate a hypothesis. Test it. One platform.' This is coherent and memorable.
findings.concern_path: If the cold-open, R01 hook, cached/live caveats, and reviewer beat are all inserted aggressively, the video can become a list of defenses rather than a clean research story. That would harm Demo while trying to improve credibility.
findings.suggested_action: Keep the title/endcap spine unchanged or nearly unchanged. Make the refusal the opening proof, but return quickly to the one-bench thesis. Treat caveats as precision labels, not apologies.

findings.title: Harmful-Fix Warning: De-Numbering Should Preserve Receipt Texture
findings.evidence: The panel proposes cutting the NAB2 overlay to three figures. Frame 8's current density is excessive, but the receipt aesthetic is part of the credibility signal.
findings.concern_path: A too-clean marketing-style frame could make the result feel less auditable and weaken the project's distinctive receipt-backed identity.
findings.suggested_action: Reduce the primary row, but leave a visible 'receipt strip' or artifact filename/checksum-style cue. The fix should improve scanability without making the proof look decorative.

findings.title: Assessment Boundary: Audio Quality And Music Mix Are Not Assessable From Frames
findings.evidence: The prompt provides transcript, captions, duration, and audio metadata, but the actual cloned voice timbre, narration naturalness, and CC-BY music mix cannot be heard from the attached storyboard.
findings.concern_path: The panel flagged cloned-voice quality as an unresolved delivery bet. I can infer pacing pressure from duration and text density, but not whether the voice or music helps or hurts the demo.
findings.suggested_action: Before final render/public upload, have at least one listener check voice naturalness, intelligibility over the -20 dB music bed, and whether lower-thirds appear long enough to read. This is a QA item, not a content rewrite.

convergence_status: round 1 — no prior position to converge with
recommended_next_question: Claude should address whether it accepts the video-visible reprioritization: first fix the repo-integrity P0s, then recut around an early IL2 refusal, legible live-proof callouts, scoped disease-hop wording, and a less dense NAB2 receipt frame, while preserving the 'library and lab on one bench' spine.
